mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in >65% of periods where FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer demand, which reduces corporate profit expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power when UNRATE growth exceeds 0.5pp.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth combined with stable consumer price inflation indicates robust consumer demand without triggering aggressive monetary tightening, supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative returns when CPIAUCSL month-over-month growth exceeds 0.4%, or interaction term IC falls below 0.01 over rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and rising business investment, which generates positive short-term S&P 500 forward returns as earnings revisions turn upward.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration fails to predict positive 5-day returns during periods of supply chain bottlenecks, or Rank IC drops below 0.03 in recovery regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts signals weakening residential investment and reduced construction activity, which drags on economic growth forecasts and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >55% of triggered months, or the threshold effect vanishes when HOUST data revisions exceed 10%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in the federal funds rate and consumer price inflation indicate a stagflationary monetary response that compresses equity multiples and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The interaction signal yields positive returns when inflation expectations remain anchored below 2%, or joint IC falls below 0.02 over 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising household income, which supports corporate revenue growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth fails to predict positive 60-day returns when labor force participation declines concurrently, or Rank IC remains below 0.03 over rolling 4-year periods.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth exceeding trend indicates strong aggregate demand and corporate earnings momentum, which translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 5 trading days as markets price in growth.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth fails to generate positive 5-day returns during late-cycle phases, or the signal IC reverses sign when real interest rates exceed 3%.
